info.name: Athena Auth API
info.description: Postman collection for Athena user auth API: register, login, refresh, logout with positive and negative tests.

request.description: Creates a new user and returns access + refresh tokens.
Authenticates an existing user and returns tokens.
Expect 401 for invalid credentials.

exec: pm.test('Has error body', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('error'); });

request.description: Rotate refresh token and return a new access token.
Uploads a PNG avatar image, stores it, uploads to IPFS and pins it. Returns updated user with avatar identifiers.

request.description: Expects 400 error when uploading PDF file (invalid extension).
Expects 400 error when uploading executable file disguised as PNG.
Expects 400 error when no file is provided.
Expects 401 error when Authorization header is missing.
Expect 400 when refresh_token missing.

exec: pm.test('Has error body', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('error'); });

request.description: Expect 401 for invalid/expired refresh token.

exec: pm.test('Has error body', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('error'); });

request.description: Logs out the current user by revoking tokens and sessions.
After logout, the previous refresh token should be invalid.

exec: pm.test('Has error body', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('error'); });

request.description: Expect 401 when Authorization header is missing.

exec: pm.test('Has error body or text', () => { try { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('error'); } catch (e) { pm.response.to.have.body(/Missing authorization header|Invalid token|Unauthorized/i); } });
